sources.label: NHS service manual confirmation page
borrowed: Keep the main transactional state concise and outcome-led.
Put what happens next immediately after the main state.
Keep reassurance calm but only when the underlying state is truly settled.
rejected: Using confirmed-page calmness before consent, proof, and continuity are all current.

sources.label: WAI-ARIA APG dialog (modal) pattern
borrowed: Move focus into the drawer on open and return it to the invoker on close.
Keep the dialog self-contained for keyboard traversal.
rejected: Using a visually decorative drawer that does not behave like a proper dialog.

sources.label: WAI-ARIA APG disclosure pattern
borrowed: Treat evidence rows and artifact expansion as disclosures with explicit toggles.
rejected: Hiding critical proof lanes behind hover-only or implicit expansion.

sources.label: WCAG technique ARIA22 status messages
borrowed: Use status messaging for non-blocking pending and watch states.
Reserve alert treatment for blocked consent and blocking drift.
rejected: Announcing every dispatch micro-state as an interruptive alert.

sources.label: Playwright best practices
borrowed: Test rendered behavior with stable selectors and seeded scenarios.
rejected: Basing proofs on hidden implementation details.

sources.label: Playwright accessibility testing
borrowed: Use accessibility snapshots as one proof layer alongside keyboard and landmark checks.
rejected: Treating a snapshot alone as sufficient accessibility coverage.

sources.label: Playwright Trace Viewer
borrowed: Capture trace evidence for the staff drawer flow where focus and disclosure states matter.
rejected: Relying on screenshots alone for keyboard-oriented drawer regressions.

sources.label: Playwright visual comparisons
borrowed: Keep scenario-specific visual captures stable across desktop and mobile states.
rejected: Using a single generic screenshot to represent all dispatch postures.

sources.label: Linear UI redesign
borrowed: Use low-noise hierarchy and compact evidence groupings in dense side-panel work.
rejected: Flattening the drawer so far that proof rows and artifact deltas become easy to miss.
